perturbation.original_text: 16. Governing Law. The terms of this Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of Singapore . All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by 3 (three) arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said Rules. The seat, or legal pla ce, of arbitration shall be Singapore , Singapore . The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. The foregoing, however, shall not preclude the Parties from applying for a preliminary or injunctive remedy available under applicable l aws for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Governing Law. The terms of this Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales. All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by 3 (three) arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said Rules. The seat, or legal pla ce, of arbitration shall be Singapore , Singapore . The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. The foregoing, however, shall not preclude the Parties from applying for a preliminary or injunctive remedy available under applicable l aws for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The governing law is changed from Singapore to England and Wales. This creates an in-text contradiction because the clause stating 'Losses will be reimbursed according to the law of Singapore' becomes directly contradictory. One section mandates Singapore law, while another requires England and Wales law, leading to disputes about which jurisdiction should apply in case of legal issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Liability of the parties. A Party defaulting under this Agreement will reimburse the other Party for any loss resulting from disclosure or unlawful use of Confidential Information. Loss es will be reimbursed according to the law of Singapore .
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of Singapore, and losses will be reimbursed according to the law of Singapore. The changed text modifies the governing law to England and Wales, creating a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which explicitly states that losses will be reimbursed according to the law of Singapore. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the legal framework for interpreting the agreement and handling liability is now ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Ownership. As between th e parties, Discloser and Recipient, respectively, will retain all right, title and interest to any Confidential Information provided or disclosed by such party under this Agreement. The Discloser reserves the right to analyze the actions taken by the Recip ient to safeguard Confidential Information. If the Recipient refuses to share information on the actions it is taking to safeguard Confidential Information or if such actions are found to be inadequate for safeguarding Confidential Information, the Disclos er may refuse to disclose Confidential Information or unilaterally refuse to perform under any agreement made between the Parties, by which the Recipient is to receive or has received access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Ownership. As between the parties, Discloser and Recipient, respectively, Discloser will retain all right, title and interest to any Confidential Information provided or disclosed by such party under this Agreement. The Discloser reserves the right to analyze the actions taken by the Recip ient to safeguard Confidential Information. If the Recipient refuses to share information on the actions it is taking to safeguard Confidential Information or if such actions are found to be inadequate for safeguarding Confidential Information, the Disclos er may refuse to disclose Confidential Information or unilaterally refuse to perform under any agreement made between the Parties, by which the Recipient is to receive or has received access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: In the section on ownership, the Recipient's ownership rights in the Confidential Information are removed, but later in the section, the text discusses the Recipient’s actions to safeguard this same Confidential Information. By changing the start to say that only the Discloser holds the rights, any mention of the recipient acting on the information should be rendered moot, creating an in-text contradiction in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership. As between the parties, Discloser and Recipient, respectively, Discloser will retain all right, title and interest to any Confidential Information provided or disclosed by such party under this Agreement. The Discloser reserves the right to analyze the actions taken by the Recip ient to safeguard Confidential Information. If the Recipient refuses to share information on the actions it is taking to safeguard Confidential Information or if such actions are found to be inadequate for safeguarding Confidential Information, the Disclos er may refuse to disclose Confidential Information or unilaterally refuse to perform under any agreement made between the Parties, by which the Recipient is to receive or has received access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "As between th e parties, Discloser and Recipient, respectively, will retain all right, title and interest to any Confidential Information provided or disclosed by such party under this Agreement." which means that both Discloser and Recipient have ownership. The changed text replaces this with "As between the parties, Discloser and Recipient, respectively, Discloser will retain all right, title and interest to any Confidential Information provided or disclosed by such party under this Agreement.", which states that only the Discloser retains all rights. Later in the same section, the text refers to the Recipient's actions to safeguard this same Confidential Information, but this contradicts the altered ownership clause because the Recipient no longer has rights to the information, but is still expected to safeguard it, creating an inconsistency within the same section, which follows the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction". The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, showing the contradiction within the same text.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Remedies. The parties acknowledge that improper disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable injury to the Discloser, and that remedies at law for any such breach could be inadeq uate. In the event of a breach or threatened breach, Discloser has the right to seek injunctive relief (in addition to any and all other remedies available at law or equity) without the need to post a bond or other security or demonstrate the confidential nature of its Confidential Information. The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement is entitled to its reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees. Each party irrevocably waives any right it may have to a trial by jury in connection with any dispute arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Remedies. The parties acknowledge that improper disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable injury to the Discloser, and that remedies at law for any such breach could be inadeq uate. In the event of a breach or threatened breach, Discloser has the right to seek injunctive relief (in addition to any and all other remedies available at law or equity) with the need to post a bond or other security or demonstrate the confidential nature of its Confidential Information. The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement is entitled to its reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees. Each party irrevocably waives any right it may have to a trial by jury in connection with any dispute arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'without', I introduce the Discloser needing to post a bond or other security to demonstrate the confidential nature of its Confidential Information. This contradicts the premise stated earlier in the section that remedies at law may be inadequate, and now places additional burden and costs to the Discloser
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. The parties acknowledge that improper disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable injury to the Discloser, and that remedies at law for any such breach could be inadeq uate. In the event of a breach or threatened breach, Discloser has the right to seek injunctive relief (in addition to any and all other remedies available at law or equity) with the need to post a bond or other security or demonstrate the confidential nature of its Confidential Information. The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement is entitled to its reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees. Each party irrevocably waives any right it may have to a trial by jury in connection with any dispute arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that in the event of a breach, the Discloser has the right to seek injunctive relief 'without' the need to post a bond. The changed text removes 'without', thus requiring the Discloser to post a bond. This directly contradicts the original intention of providing easier access to injunctive relief and places an additional burden on the Discloser, conflicting with the premise that remedies at law may be inadequate. Since the contradicted text and changed text are in the same location, the change is in direct conflict with the original intention. The change clearly meets the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned or transferred without the pr ior written consent of the other party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Any such assignment or transfer without written consent will be void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement will be binding on and inure to the benefit of the parties, the ir successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned or transferred without the pr ior written consent of the other party, which shall be unreasonably withheld. Any such assignment or transfer without written consent will be void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement will be binding on and inure to the benefit of the parties, the ir successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the consent shall be 'unreasonably withheld', I create uncertainty in enforcement of the contract. This is because, while the agreement states it cannot be assigned, the unreasonably withheld consent implies a possible avenue of assignment in bad faith, which contradicts the intended purpose of the assignment clause. This becomes an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned or transferred without the pr ior written consent of the other party, which shall be unreasonably withheld. Any such assignment or transfer without written consent will be void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement will be binding on and inure to the benefit of the parties, the ir successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 14 to state that consent for assignment 'shall be unreasonably withheld,' which directly contradicts the original intent of the assignment clause to prevent unauthorized transfers. While stating assignments are void without consent, the 'unreasonably withheld' clause introduces ambiguity and a potential loophole that could be exploited, creating an in-text contradiction within the same section (as the Contradicted Text is the same section). The original text stated consent 'shall not be unreasonably withheld.' This makes the modified text incompatible with itself and creates a strong, readily apparent conflict.
